A. log_2(n)
B. sqrt(n)
C. n - 1
D. n

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following algorithms. Assume procedure A and procedure B take O(1) and O(1/n) unit of time respectively. Derive the time complexity of the algorithm in O-notation.

algorithm what(n)
begin
  if n = 1 then
    call A
  else
  begin
    what(n-1);
    call B(n)
  end
end

A. O(1)
B. O(log n)
C. O(n)
D. O(n log n)

parsed.stem:
Consider the following algorithms. Assume procedure A and procedure B take O(1) and O(1/n) unit of time respectively. Derive the time complexity of the algorithm in O-notation.

algorithm what(n)
begin
  if n = 1 then
    call A
  else
  begin
    what(n-1);
    call B(n)
  end
end

sourceQuestion:
Let S be a sorted array of n integers. Let T(n) denote the time taken for the most efficient algorithm to determine if there are two elements with sum equal to a given value k in S. Which of the following statements is true?
A. T(n) is O(1)
B. T(n) is Theta(n) but not O(1)
C. T(n) is Theta(n log n) but not Theta(n)
D. T(n) is Theta(n^2)
sourceAnswer: B. T(n) is Theta(n) but not O(1)

parsed.stem: Let S be a sorted array of n integers. Let T(n) denote the time taken for the most efficient algorithm to determine if there are two elements with sum equal to a given value k in S. Which of the following statements is true?

options.text: T(n) is Theta(n) but not O(1)
T(n) is Theta(n log n) but not Theta(n)

sourceQuestion:
The cube root of a natural number n is defined as the largest natural number m such that m^3 <= n. The complexity of computing the cube root of n (n is represented by b bits in binary notation) is:
A. O(b) but not O(1)
B. O(b^(1/2)) but not O(1) for any constant k > 0
C. O(b^k) for some constant k > 0.5, but not O(b^(1/2)) for any constant k
D. O(2^b) but not O(b^k) for any constant k

parsed.stem: The cube root of a natural number n is defined as the largest natural number m such that m^3 <= n. The complexity of computing the cube root of n (n is represented by b bits in binary notation) is:

options.text: O(b^(1/2)) but not O(1) for any constant k > 0
O(b^k) for some constant k > 0.5, but not O(b^(1/2)) for any constant k
O(2^b) but not O(b^k) for any constant k

sourceQuestion:
Two matrices M_1 and M_2 are to be stored in arrays A and B respectively. Each array can be stored either in row-major or column-major order in contiguous memory locations. The time complexity of an algorithm to compute M_1 x M_2 will be
A. best if A is in row-major and B is in column-major order
B. best if both are in row-major order
C. best if both are in column-major order
D. independent of the storage scheme
sourceAnswer: D. independent of the storage scheme

parsed.stem: Two matrices M_1 and M_2 are to be stored in arrays A and B respectively. Each array can be stored either in row-major or column-major order in contiguous memory locations. The time complexity of an algorithm to compute M_1 x M_2 will be

options.text: best if A is in row-major and B is in column-major order
best if both are in row-major order
best if both are in column-major order

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following C-program fragment in which i, j, and n are integer variables.

for (i = n, j = 0; i > 0; i /= 2, j += i);

Let val(j) denote the value stored in the variable j after termination of the for loop. Which one of the following is true?

A. val(j) = Theta(log n)
B. val(j) = Theta(sqrt(n))
C. val(j) = Theta(n)
D. val(j) = Theta(n log n)

An array of n numbers is given, where n is an even number. The maximum as well as the minimum of these n numbers needs to be determined. Which of the following is TRUE about the number of comparisons needed?
A. At least 2n - c comparisons, for some constant c, are needed.
B. At most 1.5n - 2 comparisons are needed.
C. At least 2n_0 comparisons are needed.
D. None of the above.
sourceAnswer: B. At most 1.5n - 2 comparisons are needed.

parsed.stem: An array of n numbers is given, where n is an even number. The maximum as well as the minimum of these n numbers needs to be determined. Which of the following is TRUE about the number of comparisons needed?

options.text: At least 2n - c comparisons, for some constant c, are needed.

sourceAnswer: B. T(n) = O(sqrt(n)) and T(n) = Omega(1)

C. Theta(n log n)

sourceQuestion:
Two alternative packages A and B are available for processing a database having 10^6 records. Package A requires 0.001*n^2 time units and package B requires 10*n*log_2(n) time units to process n records. What is the smallest value of n for which package B will be preferred over A?

A. 12
B. 10
C. 6
D. 5

parsed.stem: Two alternative packages A and B are available for processing a database having 10^6 records. Package A requires 0.001*n^2 time units and package B requires 10*n*log_2(n) time units to process n records. What is the smallest value of n for which package B will be preferred over A?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following pseudo code. What is the total number of multiplications to be performed?

D = 2
for i = 1 to n do
  for j = i to n do
    for k = j + 1 to n do
      D = D * 3

A. Half of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.
B. One-third of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.
C. One-sixth of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.
D. None of the above.
sourceAnswer: C. One-sixth of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.

parsed.stem:
Consider the following pseudo code. What is the total number of multiplications to be performed?

D = 2
for i = 1 to n do
  for j = i to n do
    for k = j + 1 to n do
      D = D * 3

options.text: Half of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.
One-third of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.
One-sixth of the product of the 3 consecutive integers.

sourceQuestion:
An algorithm performs (log N)^(1/2) find operations, N insert operations, N delete operations, and (log N)^(1/2) decrease-key operations on a set of data items with keys drawn from a linearly ordered set. For a delete operation, a pointer is provided to the record that must be deleted. For the decrease-key operation, a pointer is provided to the record that has its key decreased. Which one of the following data structures is the most suited for the algorithm to use, if the goal is to achieve the best asymptotic complexity considering all the operations?

A. Unsorted array
B. Min-heap
C. Sorted array
D. Sorted doubly linked list

parsed.stem: An algorithm performs (log N)^(1/2) find operations, N insert operations, N delete operations, and (log N)^(1/2) decrease-key operations on a set of data items with keys drawn from a linearly ordered set. For a delete operation, a pointer is provided to the record that must be deleted. For the decrease-key operation, a pointer is provided to the record that has its key decreased. Which one of the following data structures is the most suited for the algorithm to use, if the goal is to achieve the best asymptotic complexity considering all the operations?

sourceQuestion:
Match the algorithms with their time complexities:

Algorithms               Time Complexity
P. Tower of Hanoi with n disks     i.   Theta(2^n)
Q. Binary Search given n sorted numbers  ii.  Theta(n log n)
R. Heap sort given n numbers in the worst case iii. Theta(n^2)
S. Addition of two n x n matrices     iv.  Theta(log n)

A. P -> (ii), Q -> (iv), R -> (iii), S -> (i)
B. P -> (i), Q -> (iv), R -> (ii), S -> (iii)
C. P -> (i), Q -> (iii), R -> (ii), S -> (iv)
D. P -> (ii), Q -> (iii), R -> (iv), S -> (i)

parsed.stem:
Match the algorithms with their time complexities:

Algorithms               Time Complexity
P. Tower of Hanoi with n disks     i.   Theta(2^n)
Q. Binary Search given n sorted numbers  ii.  Theta(n log n)
R. Heap sort given n numbers in the worst case iii. Theta(n^2)
S. Addition of two n x n matrices     iv.  Theta(log n)

A. both O(N) and Omega(N)

C. Omega(N) but not O(N)
D. neither O(N) nor Omega(N)

sourceQuestion:
Exponentiation is a heavily used operation in public key cryptography. Which of the following options is the tightest upper bound on the number of multiplications required to compute x^n, given x and n, where 0 <= m <= floor(n/2) and n = 2^m?
A. O(log n)
B. O(sqrt(n))
C. O(n^(1/3))
D. O(n)

sourceQuestion:
Let P_1, P_2, ..., P_n be n points in the xy-plane such that no three of them are collinear. For every pair of points P_i and P_j, let L_{ij} be the line passing through them. Let L_ab be the line with the steepest gradient (slope) among all the C(n,2) lines. The time complexity of the best algorithm for finding P_a and P_b is
A. Theta(log n)
B. Theta(n)
C. Theta(n log n)
D. Theta(n^2)

A. 1 2 3 4 5 6
B. 1 3 2 4 5 6
C. 1 3 2 4 6 5
D. 3 2 1 6 5

sourceQuestion:
Consider the directed graph below given (nodes P, Q, R, S with edges: P->Q, P->S, Q->R, S->R based on the figure). Which one of the following is TRUE?
A. The graph does not have any topological ordering.
B. Both PQRS and SRQP are topological orderings.
C. Both PSRQ and SPRQ are topological orderings.
D. PSRQ is the only topological ordering.
sourceAnswer: C. Both PSRQ and SPRQ are topological orderings.

parsed.stem: Consider the directed graph below given (nodes P, Q, R, S with edges: P->Q, P->S, Q->R, S->R based on the figure). Which one of the following is TRUE?

options.text: The graph does not have any topological ordering.
Both PQRS and SRQP are topological orderings.
Both PSRQ and SPRQ are topological orderings.
PSRQ is the only topological ordering.

sourceQuestion:

Which of the following is/are valid vertex orderings obtained from a topological sort of the DAG?
A. PQRSTUV
B. PRQVSUT
C. PQRSVTU
D. PRQSVTU
